examples: He picked up the book and started to read

meanings.definition: give a passenger or a hitchhiker a lift
examples: We picked up a hitchhiker on the highway
You can get the results on Monday
She picked up the children at the day care center
They pick up our trash twice a week

meanings.definition: get to know or become aware of, usually accidentally
examples: I learned that she has two grown-up children
I see that you have been promoted

meanings.definition: get in addition, as an increase
examples: The candidate picked up thousands of votes after his visit to the nursing home
the police nabbed the suspected criminals
I picked up some food for a snack
pick up a signal

meanings.definition: lift out or reflect from a background
examples: The scarf picks up the color of the skirt
His eyes picked up his smile
he always tries to pick up girls in bars

meanings.definition: fill with high spirits; fill with optimism
examples: Music can uplift your spirits

meanings.definition: improve significantly; go from bad to good
examples: Her performance in school picked up

meanings.definition: perceive with the senses quickly, suddenly, or momentarily
examples: I caught the aroma of coffee
He caught the allusion in her glance
ears open to catch every sound
The dog picked up the scent
Catch a glimpse

meanings.definition: eat by pecking at, like a bird

examples: I picked up after a nap
